# UC-13.7.2 · Fluentd and Fluent Bit Output Plugin Error Rate by Destination, Plugin, and HTTP Status Class (HEC 4xx and 5xx, Elasticsearch 429, Kafka leader metadata, S3 SlowDown, per-output_id isolation, retryable versus fatal classification)

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Reliability, Fault &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We watch your log couriers route packages to many warehouses at once. When one warehouse stamps rejections on the label, we need the exact warehouse, the reason code, and whether a later truck should succeed, so we never blame the wrong crew while a critical delivery lane quietly fails.*

---

## Description

Detects elevated Fluentd and Fluent Bit output plugin error rates per destination by fusing engine logs, Fluent Bit stderr patterns, Prometheus-style output error and emit counters, and optional Splunk internal HEC access status classes, then computes five-minute error_rate_pct, retryable_class_pct, distinct HTTP status diversity, and recency since last successful emit activity with inventory and CMDB enrichment so teams isolate failures per output_id.

## Value

Platform and reliability leaders shorten mean time to innocence when applications look healthy yet only some log routes break, because per-destination HTTP class and retry posture separate transient downstream pressure from fatal misconfiguration. Risk and engineering governance groups receive evidence that attributes failures to the owning team and service tier without conflating unrelated pipelines on the same host.

## Implementation

Land fluentd:logs, fluent_bit:logs, mstats fluentd_output_status_num_errors and fluentd_output_status_emit_count, and optional _internal splunkd_access HEC mirrors; normalize output_id; maintain fluentd_output_pipeline_inventory.csv; schedule observability_uc_13_7_2_fluent_output_error_rate_by_destination every five to ten minutes with earliest=-4h@h; join cmdb fluentd_pipeline_metadata; route critical and high severities to platform logging on-call; archive weekly evidence exports.

## Evidence

Saved search observability_uc_13_7_2_fluent_output_error_rate_by_destination with five-to-ten minute schedule; platform logging dashboard drilldowns on multisearch lanes; alert routing to Head of Platform delegated on-call; weekly CSV exports archived to a restricted evidence index.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

In a lab logging namespace, revoke a non-production Splunk HTTP Event Collector token while Fluentd logs and metrics scrapes continue, confirm multisearch arms surface rising error_rate_pct with HTTP 401 diversity on the affected output_id within two evaluation windows, and verify observability_uc_13_7_2_fluent_output_error_rate_by_destination returns critical with non-null recommended_action.

### Negative scenario

After restoring a valid token and allowing emit metrics to return to baseline with quiet engine logs for the same output_id, confirm severities return to low or medium and filtering to critical plus high yields zero rows for thirty consecutive scheduled runs.

## Detailed Implementation

### Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with observability pipeline engineering, Kubernetes platform teams, and the service owners who contract each logging destination. This use case isolates output plugin error rates per destination with explicit breakdown of HTTP and protocol-facing status classes, then classifies retryable backpressure versus fatal misconfiguration so incidents do not collapse into a vague logging outage narrative. Fluentd and Fluent Bit are multi-output systems: a single host can simultaneously ship to Splunk ingestion, Elasticsearch, Kafka, object storage, and vendor-specific observability endpoints. Without per-output_id isolation, a noisy secondary sink can drown the signal for a business-critical primary sink, and the wrong team gets paged while the real contract owner remains unaware.

Fluentd log severity semantics matter for triage. fluent.warn lines often precede hard failures and include rich context such as plugin_id, chunk_id, and response metadata when outputs encode it. fluent.error lines typically indicate the engine considers the condition serious enough to surface to operators immediately, yet some distributions map transport failures into warn while schema validation failures map into error. Treat the presence of error_class fields and structured exception classes as authoritative when your deployment emits them, and treat free-text phrases such as failed to flush or emit transaction failed as compatibility signals across gem versions. Secondary output definitions and copy trees mean one slow destination must not poison interpretation of unrelated destinations: always carry output_id forward from configuration management snapshots and from monitor agents when available.

Fluent Bit uses bracketed log lines such as output plugin errors with task retry guidance. Retry_Limit and scheduler settings change whether a failure becomes a sustained storm or a short blip. Calyptia distributions may add drop_on_failure semantics that convert some classes into deliberate loss with explicit counters; when enabled, your error rate math must separate dropped records from retried records using vendor fields rather than assuming every failure is a retry loop. Splunk Connect for Kubernetes provides a reference chart layout for HEC destinations, buffer paths, and Kubernetes metadata enrichment. Compare any bespoke Fluentd deployment against that reference when you suspect ConfigMap drift, mixed gem versions, or incompatible buffer plugins between Fluentd major versions.

HTTP status classes provide a coarse but powerful retry contract when present. For Splunk HTTP Event Collector semantics, 400 often indicates malformed events or payload rules rejection, 401 and 403 indicate token and authorization problems that are usually fatal until credentials change, and 503 indicates service unavailable conditions that are commonly retryable when load returns to normal. Always pair status codes with response bodies when your forwarder logs them, because some gateways return 502 for upstream authentication failures that are not retryable without configuration change. Elasticsearch bulk indexing frequently surfaces 429 when thread pools reject work or when index lifecycle management and rollover contention create temporary throttle windows; treat 429 as retryable throttling in most estates, but validate index templates and mapping explosions that make 429 permanent until schema is repaired. Amazon S3 style endpoints return SlowDown style throttling under request rate pressure; these are retryable with backoff but can become sustained during regional incidents.

Kafka producer errors include metadata states such as LEADER_NOT_AVAILABLE and broker-not-available classes that often correlate with controller elections, broker restarts, or disk-full conditions that prevent partition leadership from stabilizing. These errors are usually retryable until leadership returns, but they become fatal when topic ACLs, principal mismatch, or protocol version skew block progress entirely. Universal forwarder TCP paths and classic forwarder listeners can show connection resets when load balancers drain nodes or when acknowledgment settings mismatch; do not assume HTTPS semantics apply to raw TCP sessions.

Treasure Data td-agent packages emphasize operational stability and long-term support tracks. When troubleshooting td-agent, validate systemd unit health, disk space for file-backed buffers, and gem pins that prevent accidental major upgrades during unattended updates. at_least_once_delivery_guarantee influences how aggressively the engine retains chunks when outputs flap; combine that guarantee with retry_forever and retry_max_interval policies so operators understand whether the system will wedge on a permanently bad destination versus eventually surfacing a secondary failure mode.

RBAC and secrets hygiene remain mandatory. HEC tokens, Elasticsearch API keys, Kafka SASL passwords, and S3 signing credentials must rotate with telemetry that correlates rotation timestamps to error spikes. Restrict who can read internal access logs that reveal token identifiers. Redact customer payloads from pager templates while retaining enough HTTP class and output_id detail for decisive routing.

Scope boundaries keep sibling gold cards distinct. UC-13.7.1 focuses on buffer saturation and retry exhaustion mechanics rather than per-destination HTTP class storytelling. UC-13.7.4 focuses on worker crashes and supervisor respawn loops where process death can interrupt outputs in ways that resemble network failure. UC-13.7.5 focuses on proactive buffer fill slopes before hard overflow. UC-13.7.9 focuses on TLS trust and handshake failures rather than application-layer HTTP codes. UC-13.3.5 focuses on alert storms across notification fanout, not forwarder plugin error counts. When multiple cards fire, merge timelines only after you confirm whether the primary signal is process health, trust, buffer capacity, or destination-specific rejection.

Risk briefing: short bursts of 429 during downstream maintenance may be benign when change metadata is present; require sustain gates before paging customer-facing teams. Mixed plugin versions after partial ConfigMap rollout can produce incompatible buffer formats that surface as repeated flush exceptions; treat sudden error_rate_pct elevation after deploy windows as configuration-first hypotheses. Log de-duplication for repeated bursts prevents runaway ticket volume when the same chunk retries and logs identical lines; prefer summarized five-minute buckets and distinct status counts rather than raw line counts for executive views.

Differentiation recap: per-output_id and per-destination error_rate_pct with HTTP status class diversity, retryable versus fatal classification heuristics, multisource fusion across Fluentd logs, Fluent Bit logs, Prometheus-style mstats counters, and optional Splunk internal HEC access mirrors, enriched with pipeline inventory ownership and CMDB metadata joins, not buffer-only saturation, not TLS-only handshake cards, not alert storm economics.

Additional depth for international gold standard operations: align pager language with accountable service boundaries by requiring every output_id row to map to a single owner_team in fluentd_output_pipeline_inventory.csv even when copy trees duplicate traffic. When finance or audit stakeholders ask for evidence, export the scheduled search results with business_priority and sla_class columns so leadership can see whether a failing route was tier-one customer telemetry or a non-production mirror. For multinational deployments, ensure time zones and daylight transitions do not skew five-minute buckets relative to vendor maintenance windows; keep _time in UTC at ingest and teach incident bridges to read UTC consistently.

Model retryable versus fatal heuristics explicitly in runbooks. Retryable classes typically include HTTP 429, 502, 503 when caused by transient saturation, Kafka metadata transitions, and S3 SlowDown signatures. Fatal classes typically include HTTP 401 and 403 when tokens are wrong, HTTP 400 when schema or formatting is incompatible until code changes, and persistent 404 when URLs point at deleted endpoints. Some organizations treat all 4xx as fatal until proven otherwise; encode that policy in recommended_action tokens rather than silently muting legitimate throttle signals.

Secondary chains deserve explicit mention. Fluentd secondary type outputs can mask primary failures when misconfigured, because retries may appear to succeed while secondary routes drop events silently. Validate secondary_only flags and suppress_retry_interval behaviors during design reviews. Fluent Bit forwarding chains through an intermediary can change HTTP status interpretation when the intermediary returns 200 while the ultimate sink rejects asynchronously; when that pattern exists, push deeper status reporting via structured logs at the final hop.

Capacity governance should pair this card with downstream ownership dashboards. Elasticsearch hot-thread storms often require index management action rather than collector tuning. Kafka disk-full events require broker remediation before producer tuning. Splunk ingestion saturation may require token partitioning and indexer scaling rather than raising Retry_Limit without bounds.

### Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up five multisearch arms so a silent single lane cannot hide a genuine delivery crisis during partial outages.

Arm Fluentd logs lands engine and supervisor-adjacent lines in index=fluentd with sourcetype=fluentd:logs. Preserve multiline exceptions with generous LINE_BREAKER and TRUNCATE settings. Normalize output_id and plugin_id at ingest time when possible using transforms that read configuration snapshots. Extract http_status using regex anchors that avoid matching timestamps. Capture error_class tokens when present. Include kubernetes.pod_name and node fields for DaemonSets.

Arm Fluent Bit logs lands stderr streams in index=fluent_bit with sourcetype=fluent_bit:logs. Focus on output errors, upstream failures, Kafka metadata errors, and HTTP status fragments. Map section or plugin identifiers into output_id consistently with inventory rows. Strip ANSI color codes at index time.

Arm Fluentd Prometheus errors uses mstats against metric_name fluentd_output_status_num_errors with dimensions for host and output_id. Align scrape intervals with log collection windows so five-minute buckets correlate without systematic skew. Document cardinality controls when output_id includes high-cardinality labels; aggregate upstream if security requires.

Arm Fluentd Prometheus emits uses mstats against metric_name fluentd_output_status_emit_count for the same dimensions. This arm supplies the denominator family for error_rate_pct alongside error numerators from logs and metrics. If your exporter uses different names, map them in macros rather than silently dropping arms.

Arm Splunk internal HEC access mirrors optional destination-side evidence from index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd_access for collector paths, emphasizing 4xx and 5xx classes when governance permits. Restrict roles tightly because these logs reveal topology and token usage patterns.

Maintain fluentd_output_pipeline_inventory.csv keyed on output_id with destination, plugin, owner_team, sla_class, runbook_url, and business_priority. Refresh weekly or automate from service catalog exports. Maintain cmdb sourcetype=fluentd_pipeline_metadata with deduped output_id rows for supplemental ownership and pipeline_region hints when inventory lags during migrations.

props.conf should honor millisecond timestamps, preserve UTF-8 payloads for international tenants, and avoid aggressive SEDCMD that removes HTTP status tokens. transforms.conf should copy kubernetes labels into host only when host is empty to prevent duplicate entities.

Pre-save validation: each arm must return rows within one hour in lab. Induce a harmless non-production token revocation and confirm Fluentd logs and internal access mirrors move together. Pause a lab Kafka broker and confirm LEADER_NOT_AVAILABLE style language appears in Fluent Bit logs while metrics arms move.

Example operator helpers for design reviews:

```bash
kubectl -n logging logs daemonset/fluent-bit --tail=200
kubectl -n logging get configmap -o yaml | rg -n "output|match|forward|http"
curl -sS http://127.0.0.1:24220/api/plugins.json | head
```

Dashboards: heatmap error_rate_pct by owner_team and destination, sparkline distinct_http_status_count, drilldowns to raw engine lines and inventory runbook_url links.

Extended collection guidance for hybrid estates: when some clusters use DaemonSets and others use virtual machine agents, keep host keys stable by preferring kubernetes.node_name for Kubernetes and fqdn for virtual machines, then coalesce in the search rather than renaming upstream inconsistently. For air-gapped environments, internal HEC access mirrors may be the fastest proof of gateway health when external status pages are unavailable; document legal retention for those logs explicitly.

### Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as observability_uc_13_7_2_fluent_output_error_rate_by_destination with a five-minute schedule in dense fleets and a ten-minute schedule when multisearch cost requires breathing room, using dispatch earliest=-4h@h and latest=now. Page when severity is critical, warn when high, and route medium to platform chat with output_id in the title. Throttle duplicate host, output_id pairs for twenty minutes unless severity escalates. Include recommended_action tokens in pager bodies so incident commanders open runbooks without rerunning ad hoc searches.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans Fluentd logs, Fluent Bit logs, Fluentd error metrics, Fluentd emit metrics, and optional internal HEC access evidence. coalesce ladders absorb missing host, output_id, plugin, and destination keys introduced by heterogeneous exporters. bin span=5m aligns buckets before stats aggregates error_count_5min and emit_count_5min. error_rate_pct uses error_count divided by the sum of errors and emits for the bucket, emphasizing delivery failure intensity rather than raw log volume. streamstats window=2 approximates sustained elevation across consecutive buckets for policy gates. streamstats on emit buckets estimates recency of successful emit activity for time_since_last_success. eventstats perc95 by owner_team provides a team-relative baseline for high severity without demanding static global thresholds. join attaches inventory ownership and CMDB metadata for per-pipeline isolation. case maps HTTP class diversity, retryable proportion, sustained elevation, and success recency into critical, high, medium, and low tiers.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.7.2 Fluentd Fluent Bit per-destination output error rate HTTP class retryable-vs-fatal multisearch earliest=-4h")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=fluentd sourcetype=fluentd:logs earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where match(lr,"error_class=|failed to flush|fluent\.warn|emit transaction failed|response code|out_http|non-200|rejected")
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,kubernetes.pod_name,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval output_id=trim(toString(coalesce(output_id,plugin_id,out_id,match_output,"unmapped_output")))
      | eval plugin=trim(toString(coalesce(plugin,plugin_type,out_type,"unknown_plugin")))
      | eval destination=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(destination,dest_url,hec_url,forward_host,"unknown_destination"))))
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "(?i)(?<http_status>\d{3})(?!\d)"
      | eval http_status=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(http_status,status,code,"0")),10)
      | eval err_wt=1
      | eval emit_wt=0
      | eval retryable_hit=if(http_status==429 OR http_status>=500 OR match(lr,"leader.?not.?available|slow.?down|throttl|timeout|connection reset|broken pipe"),1,0)
      | fields _time host output_id plugin destination http_status err_wt emit_wt retryable_hit ]
    [ search index=fluent_bit sourcetype=fluent_bit:logs earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where match(lr,"failed to flush|http status|kafka|elasticsearch|splunk|retry|upstream|output:")
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,pod_name,kubernetes.pod_name,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval output_id=trim(toString(coalesce(output_id,plugin_name,section,"fluent_bit_output")))
      | eval plugin=trim(toString(coalesce(plugin,type,"fluent_bit")))
      | eval destination=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(destination,host_dest,upstream,"unknown_destination"))))
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "(?i)HTTP/(?:1\\.[01]|2)\\s+(?<http_status>\\d{3})"
      | eval http_status=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(http_status,code,"0")),10)
      | eval err_wt=1
      | eval emit_wt=0
      | eval retryable_hit=if(http_status==429 OR http_status>=500 OR match(lr,"leader.?not.?available|slow.?down|retri|throttl|unavailable"),1,0)
      | fields _time host output_id plugin destination http_status err_wt emit_wt retryable_hit ]
    [ | mstats sum(_value) AS err_v WHERE index=metrics metric_name="fluentd_output_status_num_errors" span=5m BY host output_id
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval output_id=trim(toString(output_id))
      | rex field=output_id "(?<plugin_hint>[^:/]+)"
      | eval plugin=coalesce(plugin_hint,"fluentd_metrics")
      | eval destination="metrics_destination"
      | eval http_status=0
      | eval err_wt=err_v
      | eval emit_wt=0
      | eval retryable_hit=round(err_v*0.55,0)
      | fields _time host output_id plugin destination http_status err_wt emit_wt retryable_hit ]
    [ | mstats sum(_value) AS emit_v WHERE index=metrics metric_name="fluentd_output_status_emit_count" span=5m BY host output_id
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval output_id=trim(toString(output_id))
      | rex field=output_id "(?<plugin_hint>[^:/]+)"
      | eval plugin=coalesce(plugin_hint,"fluentd_metrics")
      | eval destination="metrics_destination"
      | eval http_status=0
      | eval err_wt=0
      | eval emit_wt=emit_v
      | eval retryable_hit=0
      | fields _time host output_id plugin destination http_status err_wt emit_wt retryable_hit ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd_access earliest=-4h@h latest=now "/services/collector*"
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,splunk_server,"hec_peer"))))
      | eval output_id="hec_internal_mirror"
      | eval plugin="splunk_hec"
      | eval destination="/services/collector"
      | eval http_status=tonumber(tostring(status),10)
      | eval err_wt=if(http_status>=400 AND http_status!=404,1,0)
      | eval emit_wt=0
      | eval retryable_hit=if(http_status==429 OR http_status>=500,1,0)
      | where err_wt>0
      | fields _time host output_id plugin destination http_status err_wt emit_wt retryable_hit ]
| eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,"unknown_host"))))
| eval output_id=trim(toString(coalesce(output_id,"unknown_output")))
| eval plugin=trim(toString(coalesce(plugin,"unknown_plugin")))
| eval destination=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(destination,"unknown_destination"))))
| eval http_status=coalesce(http_status,0)
| eval merge_key=strcat(host,"|",output_id,"|",destination,"|",plugin)
| bin _time span=5m
| stats sum(err_wt) AS error_count_5min sum(emit_wt) AS emit_count_5min sum(retryable_hit) AS retryable_err_sum dc(eval(if(http_status>=100 AND http_status<=599,http_status,null()))) AS distinct_http_status_count max(_time) AS bucket_end BY _time merge_key host output_id plugin destination
| eval denom=error_count_5min+emit_count_5min
| eval error_rate_pct=if(denom>0, round(100*error_count_5min/denom,3), 0)
| eval retryable_class_pct=if(error_count_5min>0, round(100*retryable_err_sum/error_count_5min,3), 0)
| sort 0 merge_key + _time
| streamstats window=2 current=t global=f sum(eval(if(error_rate_pct>5,1,0))) AS sustain_gt5_cnt avg(error_rate_pct) AS err_rate_2win BY merge_key
| streamstats window=24 current=f global=f first(eval(if(emit_count_5min>0,_time,null()))) AS last_emit_bucket BY merge_key
| eval time_since_last_success=round((now()-coalesce(last_emit_bucket,bucket_end))/60,2)
| join type=left max=0 output_id
    [| inputlookup fluentd_output_pipeline_inventory.csv
     | eval output_id=trim(toString(coalesce(output_id,output_key)))
     | rename plugin AS inv_plugin
     | rename destination AS inv_destination
     | fields output_id inv_destination inv_plugin owner_team sla_class runbook_url business_priority ]
| join type=left max=0 output_id
    [ search index=cmdb sourcetype=fluentd_pipeline_metadata earliest=-24h latest=now
     | dedup output_id
     | eval output_id=trim(toString(output_id))
     | rename owner_team AS meta_owner
     | fields output_id meta_owner pipeline_region ]
| eval plugin=coalesce(inv_plugin,plugin)
| eval destination=coalesce(inv_destination,destination,pipeline_region,"unknown_destination")
| eval owner_team=coalesce(meta_owner,owner_team,"unowned")
| eval sla_class=coalesce(sla_class,"standard")
| eventstats perc95(error_rate_pct) AS team_err_p95 BY owner_team
| eval severity=case(
    sustain_gt5_cnt>=2 OR time_since_last_success>5 OR retryable_class_pct>60 OR error_rate_pct>5,
    "critical",
    error_rate_pct>2 OR retryable_class_pct>45 OR distinct_http_status_count>=4 OR team_err_p95>5,
    "high",
    error_rate_pct>0.75 OR distinct_http_status_count>=2,
    "medium",
    true(),
    "low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="critical" AND match(destination,"collector|hec"),"emergency_hec_token_lb_health_ack_queue_and_proxy_path",
    severity=="critical" AND match(plugin,"elastic|es"),"elastic_threadpool_ilm_hot_disk_and_bulk_rejection_triage",
    severity=="critical" AND match(plugin,"kafka"),"kafka_metadata_isr_disk_full_and_producer_ack_tuning",
    match(destination,"s3|object"),"s3_slowdown_regional_outage_throttle_and_multipart_policy",
    match(destination,"proxy|bad|gateway|502|503"),"egress_proxy_and_alb_target_health_drain_review",
    true(),"isolate_output_id_compare_sck_reference_and_buffer_retry_stanza")
| table _time, host, output_id, plugin, destination, owner_team, sla_class, error_rate_pct, retryable_class_pct, distinct_http_status_count, time_since_last_success, severity, recommended_action
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[observability_uc_13_7_2_fluent_output_error_rate_by_destination_alert]
cron_schedule = */5 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch observability_uc_13_7_2_fluent_output_error_rate_by_destination | where severity IN ("critical","high")
```

Performance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch imbalance, materialize each arm hourly into a summary index keyed on merge_key, widen alert searches on summaries, and retain this full search for investigations.

### Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins on the failing pod or host. Compare engine logs for a specific output_id to the Splunk row in the same five-minute bucket. Validate extracted http_status against tcpdump or curl reproductions only under change control.

Cross-check internal HEC access mirrors with token rotation timelines. For Elasticsearch, compare Fluent error lines with cluster thread pool rejection metrics. For Kafka, compare broker disk and under-replicated partition metrics with producer error classes.

Synthetic validation: in lab, revoke a non-production HEC token and confirm critical severity within two evaluation windows, then restore and confirm return to low.

Negative test: on healthy clusters after recovery, filtering severity to critical and high should return zero rows for thirty consecutive runs when thresholds match your calibrated noise floor.

Runbook linkage: document expected 429 bursts during vendor rate-limit campaigns and scheduled index maintenance so validators do not file false defects when calendars show approved work.

### Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — HEC token expired so all posts return 401: rotate credentials, validate load balancer health and indexer acknowledgment settings, redeploy Kubernetes secrets atomically, and compare against Splunk Connect for Kubernetes reference mounts before closing bridges.

Case 2 — Elasticsearch index lifecycle rollover stuck causing hot threads and sustained bulk 429: relieve ILM contention, validate rollover conditions, reduce bulk payload size in the output plugin, and ensure master-eligible nodes are healthy before blaming collectors.

Case 3 — Downstream Kafka broker disk-full causing NotEnoughReplicas and leader instability: expand broker storage or rebalance partitions, validate acks and linger under failure, and confirm clients recover after leadership stabilizes.

Case 4 — SaaS object storage regional outage or sustained SlowDown: enable vendor backoff policies, reduce multipart concurrency temporarily, and route to a secondary region only when architecture permits and legal holds allow.

Case 5 — Corporate outbound proxy returns 502 for TLS intercept or pool exhaustion: validate proxy health, bypass rules for logging egress under policy, and compare path MTU and session reuse settings.

Case 6 — Downstream Loki ingester restart causes bursty 503 responses: treat as retryable during restart, validate ring health and replication factor, and ensure collectors do not pin a single unhealthy ingester IP.

Case 7 — Application load balancer drain triggers 503 while instances register: align health check grace periods with collector retry policies and validate target group stickiness assumptions.

Case 8 — Per-tenant rate limit returns 429 only for one tenant index: isolate tenant routing labels, split outputs, and negotiate higher quotas rather than raising global flush concurrency.

Case 9 — Firewall ACL misconfiguration after change window drops outbound HTTPS: correlate with change tickets, validate egress allowlists for broker and ingestion subnets, and restore paths before tuning buffer limits.

Case 10 — Vendor schema rejects payloads due to new mandatory headers: fix formatters at source, pin schema versions, and avoid infinite retry loops by bounding retry_max_interval under policy while engineering deploys compatibility shims.

Case 11 — Fluentd v2 plugin incompatibility with v1 buffer after partial upgrade: standardize major versions per fleet, rebuild images under change control, and restore last-known-good configuration snapshots until CI catches mismatches.

Case 12 — Mixed plugin versions due to ConfigMap replacement during canary: enforce rolling update order, validate gem lockfiles in images, and compare against Splunk Connect for Kubernetes chart pins to eliminate drift.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists five arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, multisearch, inputlookup, and join appear; final table lists thirteen analyst-facing columns including owner_team, sla_class, and recommended_action tokens aligned to inventory enrichment.


## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-13.7.2 Fluentd Fluent Bit per-destination output error rate HTTP class retryable-vs-fatal multisearch earliest=-4h")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=fluentd sourcetype=fluentd:logs earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where match(lr,"error_class=|failed to flush|fluent\.warn|emit transaction failed|response code|out_http|non-200|rejected")
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,kubernetes.pod_name,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval output_id=trim(toString(coalesce(output_id,plugin_id,out_id,match_output,"unmapped_output")))
      | eval plugin=trim(toString(coalesce(plugin,plugin_type,out_type,"unknown_plugin")))
      | eval destination=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(destination,dest_url,hec_url,forward_host,"unknown_destination"))))
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "(?i)(?<http_status>\d{3})(?!\d)"
      | eval http_status=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(http_status,status,code,"0")),10)
      | eval err_wt=1
      | eval emit_wt=0
      | eval retryable_hit=if(http_status==429 OR http_status>=500 OR match(lr,"leader.?not.?available|slow.?down|throttl|timeout|connection reset|broken pipe"),1,0)
      | fields _time host output_id plugin destination http_status err_wt emit_wt retryable_hit ]
    [ search index=fluent_bit sourcetype=fluent_bit:logs earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where match(lr,"failed to flush|http status|kafka|elasticsearch|splunk|retry|upstream|output:")
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,pod_name,kubernetes.pod_name,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval output_id=trim(toString(coalesce(output_id,plugin_name,section,"fluent_bit_output")))
      | eval plugin=trim(toString(coalesce(plugin,type,"fluent_bit")))
      | eval destination=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(destination,host_dest,upstream,"unknown_destination"))))
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "(?i)HTTP/(?:1\\.[01]|2)\\s+(?<http_status>\\d{3})"
      | eval http_status=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(http_status,code,"0")),10)
      | eval err_wt=1
      | eval emit_wt=0
      | eval retryable_hit=if(http_status==429 OR http_status>=500 OR match(lr,"leader.?not.?available|slow.?down|retri|throttl|unavailable"),1,0)
      | fields _time host output_id plugin destination http_status err_wt emit_wt retryable_hit ]
    [ | mstats sum(_value) AS err_v WHERE index=metrics metric_name="fluentd_output_status_num_errors" span=5m BY host output_id
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval output_id=trim(toString(output_id))
      | rex field=output_id "(?<plugin_hint>[^:/]+)"
      | eval plugin=coalesce(plugin_hint,"fluentd_metrics")
      | eval destination="metrics_destination"
      | eval http_status=0
      | eval err_wt=err_v
      | eval emit_wt=0
      | eval retryable_hit=round(err_v*0.55,0)
      | fields _time host output_id plugin destination http_status err_wt emit_wt retryable_hit ]
    [ | mstats sum(_value) AS emit_v WHERE index=metrics metric_name="fluentd_output_status_emit_count" span=5m BY host output_id
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval output_id=trim(toString(output_id))
      | rex field=output_id "(?<plugin_hint>[^:/]+)"
      | eval plugin=coalesce(plugin_hint,"fluentd_metrics")
      | eval destination="metrics_destination"
      | eval http_status=0
      | eval err_wt=0
      | eval emit_wt=emit_v
      | eval retryable_hit=0
      | fields _time host output_id plugin destination http_status err_wt emit_wt retryable_hit ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd_access earliest=-4h@h latest=now "/services/collector*"
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,splunk_server,"hec_peer"))))
      | eval output_id="hec_internal_mirror"
      | eval plugin="splunk_hec"
      | eval destination="/services/collector"
      | eval http_status=tonumber(tostring(status),10)
      | eval err_wt=if(http_status>=400 AND http_status!=404,1,0)
      | eval emit_wt=0
      | eval retryable_hit=if(http_status==429 OR http_status>=500,1,0)
      | where err_wt>0
      | fields _time host output_id plugin destination http_status err_wt emit_wt retryable_hit ]
| eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,"unknown_host"))))
| eval output_id=trim(toString(coalesce(output_id,"unknown_output")))
| eval plugin=trim(toString(coalesce(plugin,"unknown_plugin")))
| eval destination=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(destination,"unknown_destination"))))
| eval http_status=coalesce(http_status,0)
| eval merge_key=host."|".output_id."|".destination."|".plugin
| bin _time span=5m
| stats sum(err_wt) AS error_count_5min sum(emit_wt) AS emit_count_5min sum(retryable_hit) AS retryable_err_sum dc(eval(if(http_status>=100 AND http_status<=599,http_status,null()))) AS distinct_http_status_count max(_time) AS bucket_end BY _time merge_key host output_id plugin destination
| eval denom=error_count_5min+emit_count_5min
| eval error_rate_pct=if(denom>0, round(100*error_count_5min/denom,3), 0)
| eval retryable_class_pct=if(error_count_5min>0, round(100*retryable_err_sum/error_count_5min,3), 0)
| sort 0 merge_key + _time
| streamstats window=2 current=t global=f sum(eval(if(error_rate_pct>5,1,0))) AS sustain_gt5_cnt avg(error_rate_pct) AS err_rate_2win BY merge_key
| streamstats window=24 current=f global=f first(eval(if(emit_count_5min>0,_time,null()))) AS last_emit_bucket BY merge_key
| eval time_since_last_success=round((now()-coalesce(last_emit_bucket,bucket_end))/60,2)
| join type=left max=0 output_id
    [| inputlookup fluentd_output_pipeline_inventory.csv
     | eval output_id=trim(toString(coalesce(output_id,output_key)))
     | rename plugin AS inv_plugin
     | rename destination AS inv_destination
     | fields output_id inv_destination inv_plugin owner_team sla_class runbook_url business_priority ]
| join type=left max=0 output_id
    [ search index=cmdb sourcetype=fluentd_pipeline_metadata earliest=-24h latest=now
     | dedup output_id
     | eval output_id=trim(toString(output_id))
     | rename owner_team AS meta_owner
     | fields output_id meta_owner pipeline_region ]
| eval plugin=coalesce(inv_plugin,plugin)
| eval destination=coalesce(inv_destination,destination,pipeline_region,"unknown_destination")
| eval owner_team=coalesce(meta_owner,owner_team,"unowned")
| eval sla_class=coalesce(sla_class,"standard")
| eventstats perc95(error_rate_pct) AS team_err_p95 BY owner_team
| eval severity=case(
    sustain_gt5_cnt>=2 OR time_since_last_success>5 OR retryable_class_pct>60 OR error_rate_pct>5,
    "critical",
    error_rate_pct>2 OR retryable_class_pct>45 OR distinct_http_status_count>=4 OR team_err_p95>5,
    "high",
    error_rate_pct>0.75 OR distinct_http_status_count>=2,
    "medium",
    true(),
    "low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="critical" AND match(destination,"collector|hec"),"emergency_hec_token_lb_health_ack_queue_and_proxy_path",
    severity=="critical" AND match(plugin,"elastic|es"),"elastic_threadpool_ilm_hot_disk_and_bulk_rejection_triage",
    severity=="critical" AND match(plugin,"kafka"),"kafka_metadata_isr_disk_full_and_producer_ack_tuning",
    match(destination,"s3|object"),"s3_slowdown_regional_outage_throttle_and_multipart_policy",
    match(destination,"proxy|bad|gateway|502|503"),"egress_proxy_and_alb_target_health_drain_review",
    true(),"isolate_output_id_compare_sck_reference_and_buffer_retry_stanza")
| table _time, host, output_id, plugin, destination, owner_team, sla_class, error_rate_pct, retryable_class_pct, distinct_http_status_count, time_since_last_success, severity, recommended_action
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Inventory.hostname) AS inv_host latest(Performance.mem_used) AS mem_used FROM datamodel=Inventory WHERE nodename=Inventory earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Inventory.ip
| rename Inventory.ip AS shipper_ip
| join type=left max=0 shipper_ip [| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_load FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
| rename Performance.host AS shipper_ip ]
| table shipper_ip inv_host mem_used cpu_load
```

## Visualization

Severity-colored table by host and output_id with drilldowns to fluentd:logs, fluent_bit:logs, mstats series, optional splunkd_access lines, and inventory runbook_url; sparklines for error_rate_pct and distinct_http_status_count.

## Known False Positives

Planned downstream maintenance on Splunk indexers, Elasticsearch data nodes, Kafka brokers, or object storage gateways routinely elevates retryable 429 and 503 patterns; require change_ticket correlation and sustain gates before customer-facing pages. Vendor-announced rate-limit campaigns and temporary throttle windows can dominate HTTP 429 classes without implying collector misconfiguration; validate vendor communications and per-tenant quotas before emergency config churn. Scheduled index lifecycle optimizations and hot-thread contention during ILM rollover can produce sustained Elasticsearch bulk rejections that self-resolve; pair with cluster health dashboards rather than blaming Fluentd in isolation. Scheduled bulk back-fill operations and historical replay jobs intentionally stress outputs and inflate emit denominators; gate alerts using replay identifiers and job metadata. Expected developer-cluster degraded mode and intentionally throttled lab tokens produce chronic 401 and 403 patterns; downgrade routing using environment labels rather than muting production estates. Approved chaos experiments that pause non-production brokers or gateways will spike LEADER_NOT_AVAILABLE and 503 classes by design; suppress using experiment tags tied to namespaces. Duplicate events from redundant forwarders can inflate error numerators until dedupe summaries land; validate indexer receipt rules and HEC acknowledgment settings. Mis-labeled output_id values break inventory joins and fabricate unknown_destination rows; refresh fluentd_output_pipeline_inventory.csv weekly. Metric exporter outages can zero mstats arms while log arms remain healthy; monitor scrape success separately from alert severity. Short-term collection gaps on a single sourcetype can skew time_since_last_success estimates; confirm collection heartbeats before declaring data loss.

- [Fluent Bit Manual — Kafka output](https://docs.fluentbit.io/manual/pipeline/outputs/kafka)
